segments.speaker: LAWYER
segments.text: Thanks for coming in. Tell me what brought you here today.

segments.speaker: CLIENT
segments.text: I hired a company called Trellis Studio to build a website for my bakery, and they never finished it.

segments.speaker: CLIENT
segments.text: We agreed they would deliver the finished website for four thousand dollars, and I paid two thousand dollars up front as a deposit.

segments.speaker: CLIENT
segments.text: They were supposed to have it done by last Friday, but they just stopped responding to me.

segments.speaker: LAWYER
segments.text: When did you and Trellis first agree to the work?

segments.speaker: CLIENT
segments.text: It was March 8th, I think. We had emailed back and forth for a couple of weeks before that.

segments.speaker: CLIENT
segments.text: Honestly, I'm not sure if we ever signed an actual contract, or if it was all just over email.

segments.speaker: CLIENT
segments.text: And the deadline — I keep second-guessing myself, but I think they promised it by the eighth.

segments.speaker: CLIENT
segments.text: I've got all our emails saved, but I cannot find the invoice for my deposit anywhere.

segments.speaker: CLIENT
segments.text: That two thousand dollars is just gone, and now I'll have to pay someone else to finish the site.

segments.speaker: LAWYER
segments.text: Okay. Let me make sure I understand the timeline and what you have in writing.
